text: **Anna:** Hello and {welcome|ようこそ/歓迎|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to my class.} to {Georgetown|ジョージタウン大学} University in {Washington|ワシントン} DC.
**Anna:** I am going back to {school|学校}
**Anna:** Georgetown is the oldest {Catholic|カトリックの} and {Jesuit|イエズス会の} university in the United States.
**Anna:** The Jesuits are a {religious|宗教的な} order.
**Anna:** {Known|知られている} worldwide for their many {colleges|大学} and universities.
**Andrew:** Students have been {studying|勉強している} here since 1792.
**Sarah:** Hey, are you finished with your {report|報告書/レポート}?
**Taylor:** Almost.
**Taylor:** I've been {writing|書いている} and {rewriting|書き直している} since last night
**Taylor:** The subject is really {interesting|興味深い} to me.
**Sarah:** Me too.
**Sarah:** I have been studying this topic for a long time
**Anna:** Am I late?
**Sarah:** You're a little late, but don't worry, the professor isn't here yet.
**Anna:** How long has the class been waiting?
**Taylor:** We've only been waiting for about 10 minutes.
**Anna:** Oh no, I forgot my {pencil sharpener|鉛筆削り}.
**Taylor:** Excuse me.
**Anna:** Do you have a pencil sharpener I can {borrow|借りる}?
**Taylor:** No, sorry.
**Anna:** Oh wait, I found my extra one.
**Anna:** Phew, that was close.
**Anna:** So, how long have you been studying at Georgetown?
**Andrew:** I've been studying here since 2015.
**Anna:** Awesome.
**Anna:** You know, I've been wanting to go back to school for a long time.
**Anna:** So here I am.
**Andrew:** Well, you picked a great {school|学校}.
**Andrew:** I've been really happy here.
**Anna:** Awesome.

**Anna:** See, I've been {working|働く/仕事をする|/ˈwɜːrkɪŋ/|I am working on a project.} for several {years|年|/jɪrz/|I have studied English for years.} now, so going back to school makes me a little {nervous|不安な/緊張する|/ˈnɜːrvəs/|I feel nervous before a test.}
**Randall:** Don't be nervous.
**Anna:** Just pay {attention|注意を払う/集中する|/əˈtenʃn/|Please pay attention to the lesson.}
**Anna:** That is great advice.

**Anna:** You know, I have been paying {attention|注意を払う/集中する|/əˈtenʃn/|Please pay attention to the lesson.}, but sometimes I still feel like I don't {understand|理解する/分かる|/ˌʌndərˈstænd/|I don't understand this problem.}
**Anna:** Like last week.
**Anna:** What's that?
**Jada:** This is my {draft|草稿/下書き|/dræft/|I wrote a draft of my essay.}
**Anna:** What draft?
**Jada:** The professor told us to bring our {drafts|草稿/下書き|/dræfts/|Students brought their drafts to class.}
**Anna:** I think she said {giraffe|キリン|/dʒəˈræf/|A giraffe is a tall African animal.}
**Jada:** No, she didn't
**Anna:** Here comes the professor.
**Anna:** Hello, class.
**Anna:** Sorry I'm late, but the {snowstorm|吹雪/雪嵐|/ˈsnoʊstɔːrm/|The snowstorm blocked the roads.} made getting here really {difficult|難しい/困難な|/ˈdɪfɪkəlt/|This math problem is difficult.}
**Anna:** I see you've been waiting very {patiently|辛抱強く/我慢強く|/ˈpeɪʃntli/|She waited patiently for her turn.}
**Anna:** So let's get started.
**Anna:** Who wants to give their talk first?
**Anna:** Oh, please, please pick me.
**Anna:** Anna?
**Anna:** Oh me?
**Anna:** Sure.
**Anna:** Thanks
**Anna:** Here is my report on {violence|暴力|/ˈvaɪələns/|Violence is a serious problem.} in the city.
**Anna:** People have been looking.
**Anna:** For a solution to the problem of violence in the city.
**Anna:** I say stop!
**Anna:** Stop!
**Anna:** They are not the problem.
**Anna:** In fact, {violins|バイオリン|/vəˈlɪnz/|She plays beautiful violins.} are part of the solution.
**Anna:** {Violins|バイオリン/弦楽器|/ˈvaɪəlɪnz/|Violins play beautiful melodies.} create {beautiful|美しい|/ˈbjuːtɪfəl/|Beautiful flowers bloom in spring.} music.
**Anna:** That can fill a {city|都市/街|/ˈsɪti/|New York is a big city.} with hope.
**Anna:** Anna, if we put a {violin|バイオリン|/ˈvaɪəlɪn/|She plays violin every day.} in the hands of every child.
**Anna:** Anna, the topic is {violence|暴力|/ˈvaɪələns/|Violence is never the answer.} in the city.
**Anna:** {Violence|暴力|/ˈvaɪələns/|Stop violence in schools.}, not violins.
**Anna:** Like I was saying, many people are saying that {violence|暴力|/ˈvaɪələns/|Violence causes fear.} in the city is a big problem, a very big problem.
